llcFilesWith: Tennessee Secretary of State (Application for Registration of Assumed Name, SS-4402)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Not filed with the Secretary of State; the state assumed-name registration is for entities (LLCs, corporations, LPs), and Tennessee has no statewide DBA registry for sole proprietors
feeBasis: The $20 Secretary of State fee registers an assumed name for an LLC or corporation. The registration lasts five years and renews for another $20.
renewalRule: Renewable in five-year terms (Application for Renewal of Registration of Assumed Name, $20), filed before the registration expires.
protectionNote: Registering an assumed name discloses the name an entity trades under; it grants no exclusive trademark right. It does carry a practical effect: because § 48-249-106 screens new names against assumed names as well as true, reserved, and registered names, an active assumed name blocks a later entity or assumed name that is not distinguishable from it.
searchNote: Assumed names registered by entities appear in the Secretary of State's business search alongside the entities themselves, since they are part of the same distinguishability records.
notes: Tennessee's assumed name is an entity filing: an LLC or corporation registers one $20 assumed name with the Secretary of State, good for five years and renewable. Sole proprietors are not part of this system, and there is no newspaper-publication requirement. Because assumed names sit in the same records the state screens for distinguishability, a registered assumed name behaves defensively like an entity name while it is active.
